bullets.text: A large-scale Russian attack on Ukraine has resulted in four deaths and dozens of injuries, with Russia confirming the use of the Oreshnik missile, which travels at over 10 times the speed of sound.

The number of suspected Ebola cases in the Democratic Republic of Congo has surpassed 900, with the World Health Organization stating the outbreak poses a 'very high' risk for the country amidst health worker attacks and shortages.
A blast targeting a train in Pakistan has killed at least 20 people; officials report the train was transporting military personnel for Eid when it was attacked by armed separatists.
Teal independent MPs are reportedly in discussions to form a new political party.
A new waterfront Sydney suburb needs a name, with Sydneysiders to be asked to help rename Bays West and a competition opening to decide the area's "look and feel."

Security failures, including escapes, fires, and stabbings, have been revealed within Australia's immigration detention network, with critics citing a "minimalist staffing model" by prison multinational MTC.
The European Central Bank is expected to raise its inflation outlook next month, according to President Christine Lagarde.
Investors are concerned about a potential surge in inflation, leading to questions about why more are not investing in inflation-protected bonds.
Donald Trump's unpredictable policymaking is reportedly causing a drag on America's economic growth.
